my_thoughts: The model works: self = desires + action plans (not power/force), multiple lines of action needed. The student asked about action (focusing on action as salvation), which shows they understand: action = solution. They'll likely ask about paradox next.
accumulated_insight: T2: Student asked about action ('But if one makes constant efforts?')—focusing on action as salvation via proposes solution and highlights intensity. They understand: action = solution. The causal_chain mechanism provides model: self = desires + action plans (not power/force), multiple lines of action needed ('This 'you' is not a power or a force, it is merely a combination of certain desires, desires to get rid of something... several different lines of action are needed to attain results, not just one line'). Pattern: total negation → positive exception → differentiation → action question → model → [next: paradox question]. They're grasping: self = desires + plans, multiple lines needed.
visible_response:
Again, that will belong to the other side of you, to what I call 'you'.
This 'you' is
not a power or a force, it is merely a combination of certain desires, desires to get rid
of something.
If you realize that something is wrong, and you formulate a desire to
get rid of it, then if you can keep your mind on it sufficiently long, it becomes a
certain plan of action;
and if this line of action is sufficiently prolonged it can attain
results.

Only, it is necessary to add again that several different lines of action are
needed to attain results, not just one line.
We have to work at the same time on one
thing and another thing and a third thing.
One line of action won't do because there
are many liabilities and the balance of things has to change in many points at the
same time.
